channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: eThekwini municipality urges relocation of repatriation services to Musina
item.description: The eThekwini municipality has issued a public appeal to cease dropping off undocumented foreign nationals at the decommissioned repatriation site in Durban, emphasizing that all repatriation services...

item.title: RSS Warns Against Ram Temple 'Conspiracies' as TN MLA Arrested for Remarks on CM
item.description: The RSS, a major Hindu nationalist organization, has issued a public warning against alleged conspiracies targeting the Ram temple in Ayodhya, claiming anti-Hindu forces are exploiting the case of all...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif's Official Visit to Turkey Focuses on Bilateral Relations and Economic Cooperation
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif arrived in Istanbul on Friday for an official visit to Turkey, marking a significant diplomatic engagement. The visit comes after he attended the funeral of Iranian supre...

item.title: Tanzania Seizes Opportunity to Promote Kiswahili Globally
item.description: The article emphasizes the growing global interest in Kiswahili and calls for Tanzania to capitalize on this trend. It highlights the need for investment in educational resources, professional trainin...

item.title: Trump's Failed State Fair and Political Struggles Highlight Public Discontent
item.description: The article critiques President Donald Trump's transformation of the Great American State Fair into a political rally, resulting in widespread cancellation of events and minimal attendance. Originally...

item.title: Hotdog Features in One Music PH's Top 10 List
item.description: The article announces the release of One Music PH's Top 10 chart, with Hotdog being one of the featured artists. Published on July 3, 2026, the list highlights popular music acts in the Philippines. T...

item.title: Federal Court Orders Forfeiture of N150m Tied to Former Rep's Corruption Case
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abuja has ordered the final forfeiture of N150m linked to Nicholas Mutu, the longest-serving member of the House of Representatives. The Economic and Financial Crimes Commissio...

item.title: Andy Burnham Commits to Pensions Triple Lock Despite Criticism
item.description: Andy Burnham, Labour's deputy leader and potential future prime minister, has confirmed his commitment to the pensions triple lock policy despite pressure from within his party to abandon it. The poli...

item.title: French Jockey Mickaëlle Michel Competes in Historic Durban July with Curious Girl
item.description: French jockey Mickaëlle Michel is set to make history by competing in the 2026 Hollywoodbets Durban July alongside Curious Girl, a groundbreaking filly who became the first female horse to win the Gra...

item.title: lahore court grants 5-day remand for suspects in foreign nationals' assault case
item.description: a lahore cantt court has granted a 5-day physical remand for ahmad raza dar and three other suspects involved in the assault of foreign nationals. the judicial magistrate azhar mahmood approved the re...

item.title: Nova Scotia Firefighters Assist in Labrador Wildfire Evacuations
item.description: Wildfires in Labrador have forced evacuations, prompting the Newfoundland and Labrador government to deploy firefighters from Nova Scotia to assist in containment efforts. The provincial Department of...

item.title: England Coach Steve Borthwick Focuses on Team Strength Ahead of Nations Championship Match Against Springboks
item.description: England rugby coach Steve Borthwick has emphasized the importance of maintaining focus and confidence as his youthful team prepares to face the world-champion Springboks in the Nations Championship Te...

item.title: Tanzania's Central Bank Focuses on Economic Stability and Digital Inclusion
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) is prioritizing price stability, financial sector resilience, and digital financial inclusion in its five-year strategy to support Tanzania's Vision 2050 economic transforma...

item.title: India Monitors Bangladesh-China Teesta River Project Developments
item.description: India has expressed cautious monitoring of Bangladesh's plans to develop the Teesta River with Chinese assistance, emphasizing its focus on regional developments. During Bangladesh Prime Minister Tari...

item.title: Elections Alberta Rejects Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' Petition
item.description: Elections Alberta has rejected musician Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' petition, which sought to ban new coal mining in the Eastern Slopes of the Rocky Mountains. The petition initially met the required...

item.title: Prior threats surface in Ottawa case involving double child homicide, arson and suicide
item.description: Police in Ottawa are investigating a tragic case involving the deaths of two young boys, aged seven and twelve, who were allegedly killed by their father, Mohammed Al-Lami, in their south Ottawa home....

item.title: Kamba Musician Solo Sita Dies After Illness at Kenyatta Hospital
item.description: Renowned Kamba Benga musician Solo Sita (Bonface Kyambu) has passed away at Kenyatta National Hospital after a prolonged illness. The news, confirmed by close friend DJ Biado, has sent shockwaves thro...

item.title: Canada's Nuclear Energy Strategy Faces Criticism for Industry Benefits and Safety Concerns
item.description: The article critiques Canada's nuclear energy strategy, highlighting concerns that it prioritizes industry profits over public safety and environmental protection. Critics argue the policy benefits nu...

item.title: President Samia emphasizes innovation to boost DITF's global status during 50th anniversary
item.description: Tanzania’s President Samia Suluhu Hassan has urged stakeholders to prioritize innovation as the Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF), known as Sabasaba, celebrates its 50th anniversary. Speak...

item.title: DSS Prosecutes Sowore for Cyberstalking and Defamation, Calls for Stricter Cybercrime Laws
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) is prosecuting activist Omoyele Sowore for alleged cyberstalking and criminal defamation over social media posts labeling President Tinubu as a 'criminal.' Direc...
